stream_name: Brenus diligence module + Sanofi/AACR deliverables + open bugs

audit_method.approach: Every deliverable ties back to an on-disk source (sha256 + mtime). The Brenus diligence module and MRTX1719 bug are named explicitly because they are the two 'never-forget' items in this stream.
audit_method.correction_lock: MBD4/ATR tier claim in Sanofi deck / manuscript must be reconciled against receipt:mbd4_atr_evidence_matrix_v2 in stream_evidence.json (current_tier=Mechanistic, tier_change=NO CHANGE).

entities.name: Brenus diligence module (BreAK CRC-001 / STC-1010)
entities.what_it_solves: Hard-coded diligence guardrails + canon-frozen blocker registry for the Brenus BreAK CRC-001 program (anchor: STC-1010 haptenated whole-cell vaccine).
entities.how_it_solves: Module holds: (1) canon-frozen blocker registry preventing silent rewrites of prior downgrades/quarantines, (2) hard-coded guardrails for BreAK CRC-001 protocol claims, (3) diligence checkpoints reconciled against the 8D remediation state and PATH A formula lock.
entities.why_it_exists: CRISPRO_REPO_ARCHITECTURE_REPORT calls the diligence module 'the most important discovery' — it isolates the Brenus-specific rules from the general SL engine so protocol drift (mFOLFOX6 ± bevacizumab backbone, STC-1010 vaccine details) can't leak into other cases.

attributes.anchor_program: STC-1010 (haptenated whole-cell therapeutic vaccine, MSS CRC)

entities.name: Sanofi pitch deck directive (v1.0.1)
entities.what_it_solves: Directive JSON that generates the Sanofi pitch deck — anchor asset SAR445953 (SGN-CEACAM5C).
entities.how_it_solves: sanofiPitchDeckDirective.json enumerates each slide's canon (asset details, evidence receipts to cite, claims allowed).
entities.why_it_exists: Prevents slide-authoring drift by making the pitch deck a downstream artefact of a canonical JSON, not a hand-crafted document.

entities.name: AACR 2026 Lead Audit Tracker (50 leads, 18 columns)
entities.what_it_solves: Structured tracker of 50 AACR 2026 leads with 18 audit columns.
entities.how_it_solves: Sheet1 CSV holds the working data; the .xlsx duplicates the sheet for legacy tooling.
entities.why_it_exists: Turns AACR outreach into a testable pipeline (per-lead audit status + assignment) rather than an unstructured funnel.

entities.name: AACR 2026 schema A master JSON
entities.what_it_solves: Master schema JSON that describes the AACR 2026 outreach schema (fields, controlled vocabularies, join keys).
entities.how_it_solves: aacr2026_schema_a_master.json is the source-of-truth schema; GTM_SCHEMA_B_BRIDGE.md documents the bridge to schema B (GTM view).
entities.why_it_exists: Prevents lead-tracker column drift by pinning schema in JSON; GTM consumers use schema B via the bridge, not the raw tracker.

entities.name: AACR session navigation guide
entities.what_it_solves: Navigation guide for AACR 2026 sessions relevant to CrisPRO outreach.
entities.how_it_solves: AACR_SESSION_NAVIGATION.md maps sessions → dates → CrisPRO relevance score + assigned attendee.
entities.why_it_exists: Turns AACR floor time into a routed lead-collection exercise instead of ad-hoc booth walking.

entities.name: Intel faceted filtering spec
entities.what_it_solves: Spec for faceted filtering over the CRM/intel corpus — how to slice leads by tumor type / axis / stage.
entities.how_it_solves: INTEL_FACETED_FILTERING.md defines each facet with its controlled vocabulary and mapping back to schema A/B.
entities.why_it_exists: Prevents ad-hoc lead segmentation queries by making the facet catalogue explicit.

entities.name: BreAK CRC-001 benchmark calibration (PDF)
entities.what_it_solves: Benchmark calibration document for the BreAK CRC-001 trial — how CrisPRO's predictions map to the trial's efficacy assumptions.
entities.how_it_solves: PDF pairs each trial arm's assumed effect with the CrisPRO-predicted axis dependencies; deltas are quarantined until PATH A recomputation.
entities.why_it_exists: Provides the diligence backbone: any Brenus claim about BreAK CRC-001 outcomes is checked against this calibration PDF, not the pitch deck.
gaps_still_open: LATIFY deltas (CT-03/DL-05/PC-06) remain quarantined until vector version resolved.
PC-02 permanently downgraded until retroactive prediction reproduction.

entities.name: Coin-toss oncology manuscript (final)
entities.what_it_solves: Positioning manuscript arguing that current oncology-drug picking is coin-toss-grade, and CrisPRO's SL engine is a principled alternative.
entities.how_it_solves: manuscript_coin_toss_oncology_FINAL.md consolidates the SL evidence and the moat_comparison record into a public-facing narrative.
entities.why_it_exists: Owned narrative around the moat; keeps external-facing story consistent with the internal evidence receipts.

entities.name: Rich-text converted to markdown (working doc)
entities.what_it_solves: Rich-text-source document converted to markdown — carries additional context (~141 KB).
entities.how_it_solves: richtext_converted_to_markdown.md preserves the original content for further audit.
entities.why_it_exists: Keeps the source of prior deliverables auditable rather than lost in a proprietary format.

entities.name: Project Data Sphere audit agent plan
entities.what_it_solves: Plan for the Project Data Sphere audit agent — what it inspects, what it emits, how findings feed back.
entities.how_it_solves: PROJECT_DATA_SPHERE_AUDIT_AGENT_PLAN.mdc lists the agent's inputs, outputs, and integration points.
entities.why_it_exists: External data-sphere assets need their own audit lane, distinct from the SL engine's KB ingestion.

entities.name: MRTX1719 → WRN axis mismap (OPEN)
entities.what_it_solves: pharmacologic_analyzer maps MRTX1719 to the WRN axis, but MRTX1719 is a PRMT5/MTAP-cooperative agent — the WRN axis is wrong.
entities.how_it_solves: Current code: `'mrtx1719': 'wrn'` at pharmacologic_analyzer.py:66. Correct mapping per Bev2026 + Engstrom 2023 (Cancer Discov 13:2412-2431) + Pettus 2025 (J Med Chem 68:6932-6954): `'mrtx1719': 'prmt5_mtap_cooperative'` — but that axis does not exist yet in the CandidateAxis enum.
entities.why_it_exists: Silent axis mislabelling propagates through the SL engine's pharmacology contribution; any downstream tier decision referencing MRTX1719 for WRN is currently wrong.

source_receipts.note: bug location — not in this KB corpus

gaps_still_open: Fix requires (1) adding PRMT5_MTAP_COOPERATIVE axis to the enum, (2) re-tiering, (3) regression tests, (4) canonical receipt for the new axis.

entities.name: CrisPRO deliverable pipeline (evidence → manuscript → pitch → outreach)
entities.what_it_solves: End-to-end pipeline that converts evidence receipts (MBD4/ATR + 9p21 audit) into downstream artefacts: manuscripts, pitch deck, AACR outreach.
entities.how_it_solves: Directional flow: canonical_receipts → intelligence reports → manuscripts (v2→v4) → coin-toss positioning manuscript → Sanofi pitch directive → AACR tracker + session navigation.
entities.why_it_exists: Prevents each deliverable from re-deriving evidence — every stage consumes the immediately-upstream artefact, not the raw data.

gaps_still_open_stream_level: MRTX1719 → WRN mismap remains OPEN (no fix commit as of 2026-06).
PRMT5_MTAP_COOPERATIVE axis is not in the enum yet.
LATIFY deltas + PC-02 quarantine still constrain BreAK CRC-001 benchmark calibration.
AACR 2026 schema A master top-level shape was captured from live parse; if downstream consumers rely on nested field paths, add explicit walks in a follow-up pass.
cross_stream_refs: See stream_evidence.json for the canonical MBD4 verdict that anchors every deliverable here.
See stream_moat.json for the governance decisions (PATH A, 8D) that quarantine some of these deliverables' subfields.
See stream_sl.json for the engine that produces the tiers cited in every deliverable.
